topic: Validate and stress-test the merge design for folding deep-loop-runtime into deep-loop-workflows as system-deep-loop: structural layout, bidirectional path-coupling repair, the system-spec-kit tooling-borrow, reference migration across commands/agents/READMEs/advisor-corpus, and whether fallback-router.ts should be wired for real GLM-5.2 to MiMo-v2.5-Pro fallback.

normalized_topic: validate and stress test the merge design for folding deep loop runtime into deep loop workflows as system deep loop structural layout bidirectional path coupling repair the system spec kit tooling borrow reference migration across commands agents readmes advisor corpus and whether fallback router ts should be wired for real glm 5 2 to mimo v2 5 pro fallback
note: User constrained this detached lineage to write only inside the lineage artifact directory, so spec.md preinit and synthesis write-back are intentionally skipped.

noveltyJustification: First evidence pass surfaced the core structural invariant and showed that command YAML actively couples both skill roots, while leaving detailed migration inventory for later.
keyQuestions: Does the proposed system-deep-loop structural layout preserve the current workflow/runtime split without creating ambiguous ownership or broken relative paths?
answeredQuestions: Structural layout should preserve workflows as public hub/mode packets and runtime as internal backend under a single system-deep-loop root.

ruledOut.approach: Flatten runtime and workflow packets into one directory
ruledOut.reason: Contradicts hub logic-free and runtime backend-only contracts.

graphEvents.label: Runtime remains internal backend under merged system

noveltyJustification: This pass added a concrete migration surface taxonomy across command execution, generated contracts, doctor, agents, advisor, system-spec-kit, and runtime docs.
keyQuestions: Which bidirectional path couplings between deep-loop-workflows, deep-loop-runtime, commands, agents, docs, tests, and scripts must be repaired mechanically during the merge?
answeredQuestions: Path-coupling repair must include command YAML and compiled contracts, doctor routes/assets, orchestrator/agents, advisor Python/TS/tests/metadata, system-spec-kit test/optimizer/docs, and runtime integration docs.

ruledOut.approach: Directory move without precomputed rewrite inventory
ruledOut.reason: Executable YAML, generated contracts, doctor routes, agents, advisor, and test config contain concrete old paths.

graphEvents.label: Executable path couplings span commands, doctor, agents, advisor, and tests

noveltyJustification: This pass converted the prior broad system-spec-kit coupling observation into an ownership taxonomy: external governance, narrow seam re-exports, path-rewritten external consumers, and dev-tool dependency hygiene.
keyQuestions: Which system-spec-kit tooling should be borrowed, wrapped, or left external instead of duplicated inside the merged deep-loop system?
answeredQuestions: system-spec-kit should keep ownership of spec-folder topology, validation, templates, memory/context save, and canonical resource-map extraction; system-deep-loop should consume these through narrow seams and update external consumer configs, not duplicate the tooling.

ruledOut.approach: Duplicate spec-kit validation, memory save, or resource-map extraction inside system-deep-loop
ruledOut.reason: Would split canonical spec-folder governance and continuity ownership.

noveltyJustification: This pass grounded the reference-migration plan against live agent mirrors, advisor tests/corpus, plugin/hook/CI paths, and graph metadata edge cases, while confirming the phase 003 staging is mostly adequate.
keyQuestions: What reference migration is required across commands, agents, READMEs, skill docs, feature catalogs, manual testing playbooks, specs, and advisor corpus entries?
answeredQuestions: Reference migration must update skill identity, structured routing fields, advisor constants/projections/corpus labels, generated command contracts, agent mirrors, hooks/plugins/CI, graph metadata edges, and live docs/playbooks/catalogs; it should freeze /deep:* command names and agent names, leave historical specs alone, and use residual-grep plus routing accuracy baselines as the closeout gate.

ruledOut.reason: Advisor fixtures need field-scoped label replacement, compiled command contracts need regeneration, graph edges need deduplication, and historical specs remain out of scope.

graphEvents.label: External migration requires staged identity/corpus/docs updates

noveltyJustification: This pass added the canonical-vs-provider model-id gap and the model-profile conflict with automatic fallback, while confirming the existing optional child 004 scope.
keyQuestions: Should fallback-router.ts be wired into real GLM-5.2 to MiMo-v2.5-Pro fallback now, or remain an offline/reference-only primitive for this phase?
answeredQuestions: Do not make fallback-router wiring a blocker for the core system-deep-loop merge or this read-only research phase. Keep it optional/operator-gated. If implemented, amend child 004 to add an explicit fallback registry/mapping surface, attach substitution policy in fanout-run.cjs or through a generic retry-exhausted hook, emit attribution events/labels, and test forced-failure GLM-to-MiMo re-dispatch.

ruledOut.approach: Treat fallback-router wiring as required for structural merge closeout
ruledOut.reason: Parent and child specs mark it optional/operator-gated, and current fanout mechanics already accept retry/salvage/manual re-dispatch for GLM failures.

ruledOut.approach: Hardcode GLM-to-MiMo substitution inside generic fanout-pool.cjs
ruledOut.reason: The generic pool knows retry state, while fanout-run owns model slugs, executor kind, lineage labels, and command construction.

graphEvents.label: Fallback router is ready but zero-caller
Fallback wiring remains optional for core merge
